title: polygala vulgaris subsp. collina
intro: Polygala vulgaris subsp. collina, also known as common or European polygala, is a perennial plant belonging to the milkwort family (Polygalaceae). The plant is traditionally used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory problems such as bronchitis and asthma. The health benefits of Polygala vulgaris subsp. collina include its ability to support the immune system, lower blood pressure, and exhibit antioxidant properties. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used as a dried root or in the form of a tincture or tea, which can be consumed to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
